title: document spawn_blocking bridge lifetime in client_task::run
description: The spawn_blocking bridge at client_task.rs lines 56-64 converts the std::sync::mpsc::Receiver<AppCommand> into a tokio channel. The bridge thread survives as long as the std Receiver is live (it blocks on rx.recv()). If client_task::run returns early (e.g. auth failure), the bridge thread continues blocking until the UI drops ui_tx. This is benign but the coupling is invisible: nothing at the call site documents that early return does not immediately release the thread. Suggested rewrite: replace spawn_blocking with a tokio::task::spawn_blocking that is assigned to a JoinHandle, held in a local variable, and explicitly aborted on early-return paths via handle.abort(). Add a single-line comment: // bridge task is aborted on early return to make the lifetime explicit.
